title: boechera crandallii subsp. thompsonii
intro: Boechera crandallii subsp. thompsonii, also known as Thompson's rockcress, is a rare subspecies of plant in the mustard family (Brassicaceae) native to North America. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Boechera crandallii subsp. thompsonii include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Traditionally, the plant has been prepared as a tea or tincture for medicinal use.
